notes: KNOWN_FAILING (daemon=bug): a NON-retryable mid-turn API failure (mock-gemini-5xx returns 400 INVALID_ARGUMENT on the main streamGenerateContent turn; a 429/500/503 is retryable and writes NO transcript line, so it cannot exercise this bug) makes gemini-cli record a type:"error" MessageRecord in ~/.gemini/tmp/<project>/chats/session-*.jsonl (PR #13300). The gemini-cli parser dispatches strictly on type — user/gemini handled, error/info/warning skipped (parser.go:100-109,63-65) — and infers turn_done only from a final text-only 'gemini' message (parser.go:208-212). So the error line is silently dropped, no turn_done fires, IsAgentDone() never trips, and with no inactivity sweep on working (stale-tool timer deleted in PR #106) the session STICKS in working. The recording confirms this: the rollout session reaches working and NEVER returns to ready (only the unrelated presession goes ready). turn_end_error below asserts the CORRECT contract (working→ready, pinned to the rollout session via same_session_as) and is EXPECTED TO FAIL until the parser maps type:"error" to a terminal signal. Recorded via the interactive driver (gemini -y under tmux: send + 15s sleep — NOT wait_turn, which would time out since the turn never completes). The failure inducer (GOOGLE_GEMINI_BASE_URL) is honored only on the API-key path (issue #15430). Observations: an aborted turn bills nothing, so model is empty and cost/tokens are zero (relaxed from the basic-turn shape).

text: A short-lived presession (heap-bump/pre-rollout proc) appears in ready first; absorbed here so the real rollout phases below don't false-match it
The real rollout session appears in ready shortly after launch
User prompt drives the rollout session ready → working before the API error lands

KNOWN_FAILING: the type:"error" line should drive the rollout session working → ready, but the parser skips it so no turn_done fires and the session sticks in working — this phase is expected to fail until the parser maps type:"error" to a terminal signal
